summary.intent: Build a React Native Expo web app with a local Express/WebSocket API server to display Claude Code sessions and events from SQLite in real-time, with features like live updates, session linking between plan and implementation sessions, and rich event display.
summary.outcome: Fully implemented the backend API server (Express + ws) and Expo web app with session listing, session detail view, bidirectional plan→implementation session linking, Pre+PostToolUse event collapsing, and scrolling fixes. All major bugs were resolved including a crash from missing optional chaining on a newly added API field.

repo: Sessions and events live in SQLite at ~/.claude/hook-handler.db via Prisma 7 + better-sqlite3
NodeNext module resolution in the root tsconfig requires .js extensions on all local imports in src/
Dynamic import in the serve subcommand prevents express/ws from loading on every hook invocation — important for performance since handler.ts runs on every Claude hook event

When Claude Code approves a plan, it fires SessionEnd for the plan session and SessionStart for the new implementation session, but never fires UserPromptSubmit for the approval message — the first prompt of an implementation session is permanently unrecoverable from hooks
Plan→Implementation session auto-linking heuristic: SessionStart within 30s of a SessionEnd with the same cwd indicates a continuation; uses the existing state.agentParents mechanism
runReconstruct needs the same linking logic as the live stdin handler, using event timestamps and a lastEnded tracker to backfill parentSessionId

code.finding: session?.childSessionIds.map(...) is insufficient — optional chaining only guards session being null, not childSessionIds being undefined. Must use session?.childSessionIds?.map(...) to handle stale API responses missing the new field.

childSessionIds derived at query time from parentSessionId — no schema changes needed. GET /api/sessions builds a childMap in one in-memory pass; GET /api/sessions/:id uses Promise.all with a parallel findMany query.
FlatList nested inside a horizontal ScrollView fights for scroll event ownership — replaced with a plain map() inside the horizontal ScrollView to avoid the conflict.
react must be 18.3.1 not 18.3.2 (18.3.2 does not exist on npm). @expo/metro-runtime must be explicitly installed for Expo web to work.

workflow: @react-navigation/stack renders screens in absolute-positioned divs on web — flex:1 inside resolves to 0 height, so ScrollView and FlatList never get a bounded viewport and cannot scroll. Fix: useWindowDimensions().height - useHeaderHeight() for explicit height + overflow:'scroll' on a plain View.

When an API response shape changes, any running server process must be restarted before the new fields are available — stale processes return old shapes and cause crashes in UI code that expects the new fields.
npm install inside a nested directory fails when the parent node_modules has a hoisted react with undefined version — use --legacy-peer-deps as a workaround.
Two-pass grouping for Pre+PostToolUse: first build a Map of tool_use_id→PostToolUse, then iterate replacing PreToolUse with merged items and tracking consumed PostToolUse IDs to skip them.

friction: react@18.3.2 does not exist on npm — caused install failure; required manually correcting to 18.3.1
@expo/metro-runtime missing from app/package.json — Expo web silently fails without it
npm install in app/ conflicted with parent node_modules hoisted react (version shown as undefined), requiring --legacy-peer-deps
Scrolling required three separate fix attempts before root cause was identified as @react-navigation/stack using absolute-positioned screen containers
EADDRINUSE errors from lingering server processes during smoke-testing required manual kill steps
Optional chaining gap (session?.childSessionIds vs session?.childSessionIds?) caused a crash that only surfaced at runtime when the old API server was still serving stale responses
open_items: Sessions that begin via plan-mode approval permanently lack a UserPromptSubmit event — the first prompt is unrecoverable; Summary column will be empty until a follow-up message is sent
The 30s/same-cwd heuristic for plan→implementation linking could produce false positives if two unrelated sessions happen to end and start in the same directory within 30 seconds
RefreshControl on ActiveSessions was mentioned in the plan but the final overflow:'scroll' approach may not support pull-to-refresh on web
No process management for the API server — users must manually kill and restart after rebuilds; a --watch mode or nodemon integration would improve DX
EventItem.tsx still renders unmatched PreToolUse events (no PostToolUse found) — these could be in-flight tool calls that never completed and may need a distinct visual treatment
